headline: A traditional Georgia camp experience built for children and young adults with disabilities
subheadline: Camp Dream GA brings families, volunteers, donors, and community partners together around Summer Camp, Camp Out, and year-round support that keeps recreation, friendship, and confidence within reach.

heroPanel.heading: Camp is built around access, joy, and belonging.
heroPanel.body: Families come here for more than dates and logistics. They come to find a place where campers are known, supported, and encouraged to participate fully in camp life.

points.label: Summer Camp
points.title: Overnight sessions
points.value: A multi-day camp experience with cabin life, recreation, worship, music, and shared community.

points.label: Camp Out
points.title: Year-round connection
points.value: Local events keep campers and volunteers connected between summer sessions.

points.label: Community
points.title: Everyone has a role
points.value: Families, donors, volunteers, and friends help make camp possible every season.

quickSignals: Camp Out gatherings through the year

impactCards.title: Barrier-free recreation with dignity
impactCards.description: Camp Dream GA creates camp experiences where children and young adults with disabilities can enjoy recreation, friendship, and new challenges in a setting designed around their needs.
impactCards.outcome: Campers get a place to belong, explore, and be celebrated.

impactCards.title: A 1:1 volunteer model
impactCards.description: Volunteer counselors, junior counselors, and medical volunteers help each camper receive focused support throughout the camp experience.
impactCards.outcome: Families can trust that campers are known and cared for.

impactCards.title: Camp does not stop after summer
impactCards.description: Camp Out events, newsletters, and community updates help keep relationships strong beyond the main overnight sessions.
impactCards.outcome: Campers and supporters stay connected year-round.

programsHeadline: Programs that shape the Camp Dream GA experience
programsIntro: The original site is organized around a few core experiences, so this public rebuild follows that same simple structure while presenting it in a more modern, mobile-first way.

programCards.title: Summer Camp
programCards.description: Multi-day overnight sessions hosted at the Calvin Center in Hampton, Georgia for children and young adults with disabilities.
programCards.outcome: The signature camp experience families plan around each year.

programCards.title: Camp Out
programCards.description: Community events outside the summer season help campers, families, and volunteers stay in touch through shared activities and local gatherings.
programCards.outcome: A bridge between camp sessions and everyday life.

programCards.title: Calendar and location
programCards.description: Families and volunteers can quickly find session timing, location details, and the practical information they need before camp begins.

communityHeadline: A community effort that makes camp possible
communityIntro: The public experience is also organized around action: volunteering, donating, learning the story of camp, and understanding how the ministry works.

communityCards.title: Volunteer
communityCards.description: Volunteer counselors, junior counselors, and medical volunteers are essential to the camp experience.
communityCards.outcome: A direct way to step into the mission.

communityCards.title: Donate
communityCards.description: Giving helps keep camp accessible for families and helps sustain the staff, supplies, and programming needed each season.

communityCards.title: History and team
communityCards.description: Families and supporters deserve to understand where camp came from, what it stands for, and who helps carry it forward today.
communityCards.outcome: More trust through clearer public identity.
bullets: See how the mission has grown

activityIntro: Activities change with each session and event, but the original site consistently points families toward a traditional camp rhythm built around participation, recreation, worship, and community.

activityHighlights.title: Swimming, outdoor play, and active fun
activityHighlights.description: Camp life includes movement and recreation in a setting prepared for campers of different abilities.
activityHighlights.outcome: Campers get room to enjoy activity at their own pace.

activityHighlights.title: Music, crafts, and shared experiences
activityHighlights.description: Creative activities help campers express themselves and participate in the daily rhythm of camp in ways that feel joyful and memorable.
activityHighlights.outcome: More chances for confidence, expression, and friendship.

activityHighlights.title: Cabin life, meals, and worship
activityHighlights.description: Camp is not just a list of activities. It is a full shared environment where volunteers and campers live, eat, celebrate, and build community together.
activityHighlights.outcome: A complete camp atmosphere instead of isolated activities.

scheduleHeadline: Families and volunteers can plan around a clear yearly rhythm

scheduleCards.title: Session 1: July 12 to July 15
scheduleCards.description: The first overnight session opens the main summer schedule and brings campers, volunteers, and families back together at Calvin Center.
scheduleCards.outcome: Ideal for families preparing early for the summer season.

scheduleCards.title: Session 2: July 16 to July 19
scheduleCards.description: The second session continues the same camp experience with another round of campers, counselors, and program activities.
scheduleCards.outcome: A second window for families and volunteers who cannot attend the first session.

scheduleCards.title: Camp Out events and supporter updates
scheduleCards.description: Community events, volunteer communication, and fundraising touchpoints help keep the mission visible outside the main summer schedule.
scheduleCards.outcome: A healthier connection between summer camp and the rest of the year.

finalCta.heading: Start with Summer Camp, volunteer service, or a gift that keeps camp accessible.
finalCta.body: This public site is now organized around the same mission-first structure as the original, while making it easier for families and supporters to navigate on any device.
